## (continued 3)

SUMMARY OF CHAPTER 7Linear Algebra:Matrices,Vectors,Determinants.Linear Systems 

The e transpose $\mathbf{A}^{\mathsf{T}}$ of a matrix $\mathbf{A}=[a_{j k}]\;\mathbf{i s}\;\mathbf{A}^{\mathsf{T}}=[a_{j k}];$ rowS become columns and conversely Sec. 7.2). Here, A need 1not be square. If it is and $\mathbf{A}=\mathbf{A}^{\mathsf{T}}$ then A is called symmetric;if $\mathbf{A}=-\mathbf{A}^{\mathsf{T}}$ it is called skew-symmetric.

For a product,$(\mathbf{A}\mathbf{B})^{\mathsf{T}}=\mathbf{B}^{\mathsf{T}}\mathbf{A}^{\mathsf{T}}(\mathrm{S e c.~}7.2).$ 

A main application of matrices concerns linear systems of equations 

(2)$\mathbf{A}\mathbf{x}=\mathbf{b}$ (Sec. 7.3)(m equations in n unknowns $x_{1},\cdots,x_{n};$ $x_{1},\cdots,x_{n};$ A and b given).
